item.title: First Test Post
item.description: This is the body of the first test post. It contains enough text to be meaningful for testing the curation and layout pipeline. Topics include technology, sovereignty, and the joy of reading a physical newspaper.
item.author: alice@example.com (Alice)

item.title: Second Test Post
item.description: A shorter post about a different topic entirely. This tests that the curation engine handles variable content length gracefully.
item.author: bob@example.com (Bob)

item.title: Third Test Post: A Long-Form Piece
item.description:
This is a much longer post that tests how the system handles full-length articles. RSS feeds from blogs often contain entire articles, not just snippets. The curation engine needs to handle content ranging from a single sentence to thousands of words. This post intentionally includes multiple paragraphs.
Second paragraph: The layout engine must render long content in newspaper columns without awkward breaks. WeasyPrint handles this with CSS break-inside rules.
Third paragraph: The loss function's L_fit term estimates page count from word count. Longer items consume more of the page budget, which means fewer total items in the selection.
item.author: carol@example.com (Carol)
